Container.Name: StandardNotes
Container.Overview:
Standard Notes – self-hosted backend for Unraid (community template). End-to-end encrypted notes, sync server, files server, auth and API gateway bundled into a single image (standardnotes/server) maintained upstream.
IMPORTANT: This template installs ONLY the Standard Notes server image. You must run a MariaDB and a Redis container SEPARATELY before starting this container. See the project README for the recommended Unraid templates and required DB/Redis settings.
Required, in this order: 1. MariaDB container (e.g. MariaDB-Official from CA), empty database "standard_notes_db" with user "std_notes_user". 2. Redis container (Redis-Official from CA, default port 6379). 3. (Optional) The companion StandardNotes-LocalStack template, which provides the SNS/SQS endpoints expected by the upstream image. 4. This template – Standard Notes server.
Pre-flight: generate THREE 32-byte hex secrets and paste them into the fields below. On any Linux/Mac shell: openssl rand -hex 32 # repeat 3x
Pre-configured: • Server port 3000/tcp (HTTP API gateway, put behind your reverse proxy) • Files port 3125 host -> 3104 container (uploads / files server) • Persistent volumes for /var/lib/server/logs and the uploads directory • DB_TYPE=mysql (internal driver value required for MariaDB — Standard Notes / TypeORM uses the "mysql" driver string to talk to MariaDB), CACHE_TYPE=redis (per upstream .env.sample)
Notes: • UNOFFICIAL community template. Standard Notes does not provide support for self-hosted setups beyond the official Docker docs at https://standardnotes.com/help/self-hosting/docker. • The web client (standardnotes/web) is optional and is NOT part of this template. Most users connect with the official desktop / mobile apps and just point them at https://your-domain/ via reverse proxy. • Always expose Standard Notes via HTTPS (NPM, SWAG, Traefik, Caddy). Do not publish port 3000 to the public internet directly.
SYNC-LOOP / DUPLICATE-NOTES WARNING: Misconfiguration of Redis, COOKIE_DOMAIN, the reverse proxy, or the image tag can trigger massive note duplication. ALWAYS test with a fresh throwaway account and one client only before migrating real notes. See the README "Sync-Loop / Duplicate Notes Guardrails" and docs/sync-loop-troubleshooting.md.
Image tag (Repository field above): defaults to standardnotes/server:latest. To pin a known-good release, change the tag in the Repository field, e.g. standardnotes/server:1.32.0. Downgrading across schema migrations can leave the database unreadable to the older image.
Container.Requires: A separately running MariaDB container and a Redis container reachable from this container's network. See the project README for details.

Config.Name: API / Gateway Port
Config.Description: HTTP port of the Standard Notes API gateway. Put behind a reverse proxy with HTTPS — do NOT expose directly to the public internet.

Config.Name: Files Server Port
Config.Description: Files server port. Upstream maps host 3125 → container 3104. Keep this default unless you know why you are changing it.

Config.Name: Uploads Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the encrypted file uploads served by the files server.

Config.Name: MariaDB Host
Config.Description: REQUIRED. IP address of your MariaDB container, e.g. 192.168.20.71. IP is preferred — it is unambiguous across networks/VLANs and won't change due to Docker bridge restarts.

Config.Name: MariaDB User
Config.Description: Database user. Must match the user you created in MariaDB.

Config.Name: MariaDB Password
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Password for the MariaDB user above. Use the password you set when creating the DB user (no need to generate a new one unless you are also creating the user now).

Config.Name: MariaDB Database Name
Config.Description: Database name. Must already exist (empty is fine — migrations run on first start).

Config.Name: Database Driver
Config.Description: Leave at 'mysql'. INTERNAL DRIVER VALUE REQUIRED FOR MARIADB — Standard Notes / TypeORM uses the 'mysql' driver string to talk to MariaDB. Do not change.

Config.Name: Redis Host
Config.Description: REQUIRED. IP address of your Redis container, e.g. 192.168.20.72. IP is preferred — unambiguous and stable. SECURITY: keep Redis on a trusted VLAN / firewall it off the public internet. The official Standard Notes self-host image does not document a Redis password env; this template therefore exposes host/port only. If your image fork supports auth, verify the env var name first before adding it.

Config.Name: Redis Port
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Redis server port. Default 6379. Test with: nc -zv <redis-host> 6379 from inside this container.

Config.Name: JWT Secret
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte hex secret. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Changing this invalidates all existing sessions.

Config.Name: Auth Server Encryption Key
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte hex secret. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Encrypts data at rest server-side. Losing this means losing access to that data — back it up.

Config.Name: Valet Token Secret
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte hex secret. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Signs short-lived upload/download tokens for the files server.

Config.Name: Public Files Server URL
Config.Description: Optional. Public HTTPS URL of the files server (attachments). Example: https://files.standardnotes.bottich.lol. Set ONLY if you reverse-proxy the files server on its own subdomain. Leave empty to skip attachments — note creation, editing, and sync work without it. Single-domain path proxying is not reliably supported by the upstream image.

Config.Name: Cookie Domain
Config.Description: Optional but STRONGLY RECOMMENDED behind a reverse proxy. Must match the public sync host (e.g. standardnotes.bottich.lol). HTTPS is required for Secure cookies outside localhost. Wrong/empty value can trigger 'No cookies provided for cookie-based session token' and a /v1/items session loop linked to runaway note duplication. See docs/sync-loop-troubleshooting.md.
